notes: Eval 2 with_skill: AskUserQuestion unavailable to subagents — assertion 2 always fails in this environment
Eval 2 without_skill: agent described correct behavior from reading SKILL.md — assertion 2 fails for same reason
All evals are narrated/simulated — no real JS project fixture exists in this repo
Eval 1 with_skill: worktree fallback to $TMPDIR now works — scored 5/5 vs 2/5 in previous benchmark

expectations.text: Creates a git worktree on a dedicated branch before making any changes
expectations.evidence: Step 1 creates git worktree with sibling-first / $TMPDIR fallback pattern.

expectations.text: Runs the appropriate audit command (npm audit, yarn audit, pnpm audit) for the detected package manager
expectations.evidence: npm audit --json run after npm detected from package-lock.json.

expectations.text: Does NOT run npm/yarn/pnpm install before the audit (audit reads from lock files)
expectations.evidence: Step 6 explicitly skipped with correct rationale.

expectations.text: Applies Critical+High severity filter by default when user hasn't specified one
expectations.evidence: Default Critical+High filter stated twice with table showing Moderate/Low skipped.

expectations.text: Removes the worktree at the end, even if no vulnerabilities were found or no PR was created
expectations.evidence: Step 10 always runs git worktree remove --force, covering no-vulns and no-PR cases.

expectations.text: Displays the js-deps help summary including supported package managers and workflow descriptions
expectations.evidence: Help summary rendered with npm/yarn/pnpm/bun and both workflow types.

expectations.text: Presents the workflow selection question (Update dependencies vs Security fixes only) via AskUserQuestion
expectations.evidence: AskUserQuestion unavailable in eval environment; fell back to plain text presentation.

expectations.text: Does not create a worktree or start any workflow before the user answers
expectations.evidence: Transcript confirms --help bypassed all workflow steps.

expectations.text: Creates a git worktree on a dedicated branch
expectations.evidence: Step 1 creates branch js-deps-20260315-143022 with $TMPDIR fallback.

expectations.text: Runs npm/yarn/pnpm install before running the outdated check
expectations.evidence: Step 6 runs npm install before npm outdated with explicit rationale.

expectations.text: Applies Patch+Minor+Major filter (includes all updates) because user said 'latest'
expectations.evidence: 'No filtering' applied; all packages including major eslint 8->9 updated with @latest.

expectations.text: Runs build/lint/test validation after updating packages
expectations.evidence: All three validation categories (build, lint, test with prefix variants) run after each update.

expectations.text: Commit message notes major version changes (e.g. 'chore: update dependencies (major: pkg vX->Y)')
expectations.evidence: Commit message: 'chore: update dependencies (major: eslint v8->v9)' — exact expected format.

expectations.text: Creates a git worktree on a dedicated branch
expectations.evidence: Step 1 creates timestamped branch with sibling-first / $TMPDIR fallback.

expectations.text: Only updates react, react-dom, and @types/react — not all packages
expectations.evidence: Only the three named packages are targeted; others explicitly excluded.

expectations.text: Only installs dependencies in directories where at least one of the named packages appears
expectations.evidence: Step 6 restricts npm install to matching directories; unrelated directories skipped.

expectations.text: Asks the user about version scope (patch/minor/major) via AskUserQuestion since no scope was specified
expectations.evidence: AskUserQuestion (Question 2) presents three scope options; transcript explains why required.
